Library.description: This service library contains all the services which support the Teamcenter ECAD MCAD Collaboration solution.
Library.name: Eda0SoaEDMDCollab

Operation.description:
This operation cancels the processing of an Eda0IDXIncrement object by the calling domain, during the ECAD MCAD Collaboration process.

ECAD MCAD Collaboration is a process of exchange of incremental design changes between ECAD and MCAD engineers, during the design process for a Printed Circuit Board (PCB). The IDX file format is a STEP (STandard for the Exchange of Product model data) based neutral format that stores electromechanical data using an XML schema.

Eda0IDXIncrement represents an IDX (Interdomain Design Exchange) file which contains the incremental changes in a PCB layout, during the collaboration between ECAD and MCAD, as a part of PCB development process.
Operation.name: cancelProcessChange
Operation.returnDescription:
ServiceData containing partial errors, if the cancellation of processing was not successful in Teamcenter. No partial errors indicate a successful cancellation by the calling domain. The list of possible partial errors are:
•    249019: The input design is invalid.
•    249029: The value provided for designUID/domainIdentifier is empty.
•    249036: Unable to cancel process change because there is no IDX Increment in process by the calling domain.
Operation.useCase: •    ECAD or the MCAD designer while working on the PCB design within the respective tool, imports an IDX Increment for review. The designer cancels the review, resulting in a call to this operation.

Operation.description:
This operation cancels the intent of publishing an Eda0IDXBaseline or Eda0IDXIncrement object to Teamcenter by ECAD or MCAD domain, during the ECAD MCAD Collaboration process. This cancellation ensures that any of the domains subsequently can again register the intent to publish the Eda0IDXBaseline or Eda0IDXIncrement objects to Teamcenter by calling EDMDCollaborationService.registerPublishIntent operation.

ECAD MCAD Collaboration is a process of exchange of incremental design changes between ECAD and MCAD engineers, during the design process for a Printed Circuit Board (PCB). The IDX file format is a STEP (STandard for the Exchange of Product model data) based neutral format that stores electromechanical data using an XML schema.

Eda0IDXBaseline represents the IDX Baseline object in ECAD MCAD Collaboration. The IDX Baseline object represents an IDX file, in which the board outline and the complete geometry information of the PCB is captured. The IDX Baseline is the agreed state of a PCB design, starting from which the ECAD and MCAD tools would make incremental changes and collaborate with each other in the PCB development process.

Eda0IDXIncrement represents an IDX (Interdomain Design Exchange) file which contains the incremental changes in a PCB layout, during the collaboration between ECAD and MCAD, as a part of PCB development process.
Operation.name: cancelPublishIntent
Operation.returnDescription:
ServiceData containing partial errors, if the cancellation of publish intent was not successful in Teamcenter. No partial errors indicate a successful cancellation by the calling domain. The list of possible partial errors are:
•    249019: The input design is invalid.
•    249029: Empty input value provided for designUID/domainIdentifier.
•    249031: The intent for publishing the IDX Object cannot be cancelled, as there is no publish intent registered by calling domain. Please call “registerPublishIntent” operation to register an intent to publish the IDX Object in Teamcenter.
Operation.useCase:
Use Case 1:
•    ECAD or the MCAD designer while working on the PCB design within the respective tool, cancels the export of IDX Baseline. This would trigger a call to this operation.

Use Case 2:
•    ECAD or the MCAD designer while working on the PCB design within the respective tool, cancels the export of IDX Increment. This would trigger a call to this operation.

ParameterInfo.description: This structure represents the input to the operation.

Operation.description:
This operation creates a new Eda0IDX business object in Teamcenter for a Design participating in an ECAD MCAD Collaboration. Optionally, it also allows creation of a package containing 3D Model files corresponding to Printed Circuit Board components contained within an Eda0IDXBaseline or Eda0IDXIncrement.

ECAD MCAD Collaboration is a process of exchange of incremental design changes between ECAD and MCAD engineers, during the design process for a Printed Circuit Board (PCB). The IDX file format is a STEP (STandard for the Exchange of Product model data) based neutral format that stores electromechanical data using an XML schema.

In Teamcenter, IDX files for an ECAD MCAD Collaboration are managed as Eda0IDXBaseline, Eda0IDXIncrement and Eda0IDXResponse objects, which are types of Eda0IDX business object.

Eda0IDX represents any Teamcenter managed IDX object.

Eda0IDXBaseline represents the IDX Baseline object in ECAD MCAD Collaboration. The IDX Baseline object represents an IDX file, in which the board outline and the complete geometry information of the PCB is captured. The IDX Baseline is the agreed state of a PCB design, starting from which the ECAD and MCAD tools would make incremental changes and collaborate with each other in the PCB development process.

Eda0IDXIncrement represents an IDX (Interdomain Design Exchange) file which contains the incremental changes in a PCB layout, during the collaboration between ECAD and MCAD, as a part of PCB development process.

Eda0IDXResponse represents an IDX (Interdomain Design Exchange) file, which is created as a part of ECAD or MCAD tool reviewing and accepting or rejecting the proposed incremental changes. The IDX Response file is then imported by the tool which initiated the proposal, to bring the design in sync in both ECAD and MCAD tools.
Operation.name: publishIDX2
Operation.returnDescription:
The created Eda0IDX and optionally Eda03DModelsPackage objects. Any failures will be returned in the ServiceData as a list of partial errors.
    • 249019: The input design is invalid.
    • 249005: The IDX object cannot be published. Please call the "registerPublishIntent" operation before publishing.
    • 249014: IDX Response cannot be published, because the IDX Increment is not downloaded for review. Please use "getCollaborationData" operation to download the IDX Increment for processing.
    • 249029: Input value cannot be empty. Please provide valid values for "domainIdentifier", "fileName", "fileExtension", "folderPath".
    • 249030: The value provided for "idxObjectType" is invalid. The valid values are "IDXBaseline", "IDXIncrement" or "IDXResponse".
Operation.useCase:
Use Case 1:
    • ECAD or the MCAD designer while working on the PCB design, use the export IDX Baseline functionality within the respective tool, to create Eda0IDXBaseline object in Teamcenter. Optionally ECAD designer may also publish the Components 3D Model Package file along with Eda0IDXBaseline.
    • The preceding Eda0IDXBaseline and any Eda0IDXIncrement business objects published by the calling domain, if existing, are marked as obsoleted in Teamcenter.


Use Case 2:
    • ECAD or the MCAD designer while working on the PCB design, use the export IDX Increment functionality within the respective tool, to create a Eda0IDXIncrement object in Teamcenter. Optionally ECAD designer may also publish the Components 3D Model Package file along with Eda0IDXIncrement.


Use Case 3:
    • ECAD or the MCAD designer while working on the PCB design in their respective tool, imports an IDX Increment for reviewing the changes.
    • The designer reviews the changes contained within the IDX file, accepts or rejects each change and publishes an IDX Response to create an Eda0IDXResponse object in Teamcenter.

ParameterInfo.description: This structure represents the input to the operation.

Operation.description:
This operation registers the intent of publishing an Eda0IDXBaseline or Eda0IDXIncrement object to Teamcenter by ECAD or MCAD domain, during the ECAD MCAD Collaboration process. This registration ensures that only the calling domain can publish the Eda0IDXBaseline or Eda0IDXIncrement objects to Teamcenter during the collaboration. If the intent to publish an Eda0IDXBaseline is successfully registered by one of the domains by calling this operation, the collaborating domain calling this operation would get a failure. If the intent to publish an Eda0IDXIncrement is successfully registered by one of the domains calling this operation, the collaborating domain calling this operation would get a success, as both domains can publish their respective Eda0IDXIncrement objects to Teamcenter, at the same time.

ECAD MCAD Collaboration is a process of exchange of incremental design changes between ECAD and MCAD engineers, during the design process for a Printed Circuit Board (PCB). The IDX file format is a STEP (STandard for the Exchange of Product model data) based neutral format that stores electromechanical data using an XML schema.

Eda0IDXBaseline represents the IDX Baseline object in ECAD MCAD Collaboration. The IDX Baseline object represents an IDX file, in which the board outline and the complete geometry information of the PCB is captured. The IDX Baseline is the agreed state of a PCB design, starting from which the ECAD and MCAD tools would make incremental changes and collaborate with each other in the PCB development process.

Eda0IDXIncrement represents an IDX (Interdomain Design Exchange) file which contains the incremental changes in a PCB layout, during the collaboration between ECAD and MCAD, as a part of PCB development process.
Operation.name: registerPublishIntent
Operation.returnDescription:
ServiceData containing partial errors or warnings, if any. No errors in ServiceData indicates that the operation is successful. The list of partial errors and warnings are:
•    249019: The intent for publishing IDX Object cannot be registered because design is invalid.
•    249020: The intent for publishing IDX Object cannot be registered because intent is invalid. The valid values are ‘IDXBaseline’ or ‘IDXIncrement’.
•    249021: The intent for publishing IDX Object cannot be registered because no IDX Baseline is associated with the input design.
•    249022: The intent for publishing IDX Object cannot be registered because IDX Baseline is reserved by other domain.
•    249023: The intent for publishing IDX Object cannot be registered because the preceding IDX increment from the same domain is being processed.
•    249024: The intent for publishing IDX Object cannot be registered because the preceding IDX increment from the same domain has an IDX response to be processed. Please download the IDX Response for processing.
•    249025: The intent for publishing IDX Object cannot be registered because there is an unprocessed IDX Increment from other domain which is not processed yet. Please download the IDX Increment for processing.
•    249026: The intent for publishing IDX Object registered successfully. However, there exists an IDX Increment published by your domain, which would be marked obsolete, if you proceed to publish the IDX object. Please cancel the registered intent if this is not intended.
•    249027: The intent for publishing IDX Object is already registered successfully. Please proceed to publish the IDX object.
•    249028: The intent for publishing IDX Increment registered successfully. However, the IDX Baseline is not downloaded by the collaborating domain. If you choose to publish a new IDX Baseline instead of an IDX Increment please cancel publish intent and subsequently call this operation with the "IDXBaseline" as the publish intent value.
•    249029: Empty input value provided for designUID/domainIdentifier/publishIntent.
Operation.useCase:
Use Case 1:
•    ECAD or the MCAD designer while working on the PCB design, open the export IDX Baseline Dialog within the respective tool. This would trigger a call to this operation.

Use Case 2:
•    ECAD or the MCAD designer while working on the PCB design, open the export IDX Increment Dialog within the respective tool. This would trigger a call to this operation.

Operation.description:

Operation.name: checkIDXToProcess
Operation.returnDescription:
Returns a structure to indicate whether the collaboration associated with the input design has a published baseline or published increment or reviewed response for the current domain. Any failures will be returned with the client id mapped to error message in the ServiceData list of partial errors:

·    249019: The input design is invalid.
·    249036: There is no collaboration session associated with the input design.
·    249037: Invalid domain is passed in the input as no such domain exists for the collaboration
Operation.useCase:
Use Case 1:
•    ECAD designer published Eda0IDXBaseline to Teamcenter.
•    MCAD tool queries to check if there is an Eda0IDXBaseline present to be downloaded from Teamcenter.
•    MCAD tool shows a notification for MCAD designer to download the Eda0IDXBaseline from Teamcenter.


Use Case 2:
•    ECAD designer published Eda0IDXIncrement to Teamcenter.
•    MCAD tool queries Teamcenter to check if there is an Eda0IDXIncrement to be reviewed.
•    MCAD tool shows a notification for MCAD designer to download the Eda0IDXIncrement from Teamcenter for review.


Use Case 3:
•    ECAD designer published Eda0IDXResponse to Teamcenter.
•    MCAD tool queries Teamcenter to check if there is an Eda0IDXResponse to be processed.
•    MCAD tool shows a notification for MCAD designer to download the Eda0IDXResponse from Teamcenter for processing.


Use Case 4:
•    ECAD designer published Eda0IDXBaseline to Teamcenter.
•    ECAD designer published Eda0IDXIncrement to Teamcenter.
•    MCAD tool queries Teamcenter to check for any Eda0IDX available to be processed.
•    MCAD tool shows a notification for MCAD designer to download the Eda0IDXBaseline and Eda0IDXIncrement from Teamcenter for processing.

Use Case 5:
•    ECAD designer published Eda0IDXIncrement to Teamcenter.
•    MCAD designer published Eda0IDXIncrement to Teamcenter.
•    ECAD designer reviews the MCAD Eda0IDXIncrement and publishes an Eda0IDXResponse.
•    MCAD tool queries Teamcenter to check for any Eda0IDX available to be processed.
•    MCAD tool shows a notification for MCAD designer to download the Eda0IDXIncrement for review and Eda0IDXResponse for processing, from Teamcenter.

ParameterInfo.description: This structure represents the input to the operation.

Operation.description:
ECAD MCAD Collaboration is a process of exchange of incremental design changes between ECAD and MCAD engineers, during the design process for a Printed Circuit Board (PCB).

This operation creates or updates an Eda0EDMDCollaboration business object in Teamcenter for an ECAD or MCAD Design Item Revision. Eda0EDMDCollaboration being associated with an ECAD or MCAD Design Item Revision represents that the Design Item Revision participates in an ECAD MCAD Collaboration.
Operation.name: createOrUpdateCollaboration
Operation.returnDescription:
The created or updated Eda0EDMDCollaboration object. Any failures will be returned with the client id mapped to error message in the ServiceData list of partial errors:
•    249040: Collaboration object to be updated is not found in Teamcenter.
•    249041: The object to be attached from Collaboration is not found in Teamcenter.
•    249042: The object to be detached from Collaboration is not found in Teamcenter.
Operation.useCase:
Use Case 1:
•    Project Leader opens an EDACCABaseRevision or Item Revision in Active Workspace representing the ECAD or MCAD design.
•    Project Leader clicks a command to initiate the ECAD MCAD Collaboration.

Use Case 2:
•    Project Leader opens an Eda0EDMDCollaboration object in Active Worskpace.
•    Project Leader clicks on the command to add designs to ECAD MCAD Collaboration.
•    Project Leader performs a Teamcenter search for ECAD or MCAD Design and adds it to the ECAD MCAD Collaboration.

Use Case 3:
•    Project Leader opens an Eda0EDMDCollaboration object in Active Worskpace.
•    Project Leader is able to view the participating ECAD and MCAD design Item Revision and selects one of them.
•    Project Leader clicks a command to remove the selected design from the ECAD MCAD Collaboration.

ParameterInfo.description: This structure represents the input to the operation.

Operation.description:
This operation deletes an Eda0IDXBaseline or Eda0IDXIncrement from Teamcenter, for an input design participating in ECAD MCAD Collaboration.

In the case of delete of an Eda0IDXBaseline using this operation, any change which was done to the preceding Eda0IDXBaseline and the associated Eda0IDXIncrement during the publish of the unintended Eda0IDXBaseline, is reverted.

Operation.name: deleteIDX
Operation.returnDescription:
ServiceData containing partial errors or warnings, if any. No errors in ServiceData indicates that the operation is successful. The list of partial errors and warnings are:
•    249019: The input design is invalid.
•    249029: The value provided for designUID/domainIdentifier is empty.
•    249038: The intent for deleting the IDX Object cannot be completed because there is no published IDX Baseline or IDX Increment which can be deleted by the calling domain.
Operation.useCase:
Use Case 1:

* ECAD designer publishes an Eda0IDXBaseline by mistake.

* ECAD designer deletes the unintended Eda0IDXBaseline by calling this operation.

Use Case 2:

* MCAD designer publishes an Eda0IDXIncrement by mistake.

* MCAD designer deletes the unintended Eda0IDXIncrement by calling this operation.

ParameterInfo.description: The UID for the ECAD design ItemRevision.

Operation.description:
ECAD MCAD Collaboration is a process of exchange of incremental design changes between ECAD and MCAD engineers, during the design process for a Printed Circuit Board (PCB).

This operation fetches 3D Models of PCB parent assembly BOM for an ECAD design ItemRevision participating in an ECAD MCAD Collaboration. This operation also fetches an assembly file which contains the placement coordinates of the 3D Models.
Operation.name: getAssemblyContext3DModels
Operation.returnDescription:
An FMS read ticket for the zip file containing the 3D Model part files and Assembly file.
Any failures will be returned in the ServiceData as list of partial errors:
•    249019: The input design is invalid.
•    249039: The design is not participating in any collaboration.
•    249045: No 3D Model part files exist for the input design.
Operation.useCase:
•    ECAD designer has made some changes to the ECAD Design ItemRevision in the tool.
•    ECAD designer uses this operation to get the 3D Models and the Assembly File for the PCB parent assembly BOM context from the Eda0EDMDCollaboration in Teamcenter.
•    ECAD designer validates the changes of the PCB using the Assembly File and the 3D Models.
•    ECAD designer publishes the changes as Eda0IDXIncrement to Teamcenter.

Operation.name: getCollaborationData
Operation.returnDescription:
Contains the objects associated with the input design participating in ECAD MCAD Collaboration, as per the inputs for the operation. Any failures will be returned with the client id mapped to error message in the ServiceData list of partial errors:
•    249029: The value provided for IDX Object is empty.
•    249031: The Object Type is invalid. The valid values are "Eda0IDXBaseline", "Eda0IDXIncrement", "Eda0IDXResponse" or "ChangeItem".
•    249032: The filter criteria is invalid. The valid values are "includeObsolete", "includePendingReview" or an empty string.
•    249033: The IDX data cannot be returned because there exists more than one IDX Baseline for the state.
•    249034: The IDX data cannot be returned because there exists more than one IDX Increment for the state.
Operation.useCase:
Use Case 1:
•    ECAD or MCAD Designer working in respective tools in the context of a PCB design managed in Teamcenter, clicks on a command within the tool, to display the latest Eda0IDXBaseline object for the collaboration.

Use Case 2:
•    Active Workspace displays Eda0IDXBaseline objects and all associated Eda0IDXIncrement for a PCB Design Item participating in the ECAD MCAD Collaboration.

Use Case 3:
•    ECAD or MCAD Designer working in respective tools in the context of a PCB design managed in Teamcenter, clicks on a command to display the latest Eda0IDXIncrement object for the collaboration which was published to Teamcenter by the calling domain.

Use Case 4:
•    ECAD or MCAD Designer working in respective tools in the context of a PCB design managed in Teamcenter, click on a command to display all the Eda0IDXIncrement objects for the collaboration which was published to Teamcenter by the calling domain.

Use Case 5:
•    ECAD or MCAD tools, in the context of a PCB Design Item, query Teamcenter to find the Eda0IDXIncrement object, which was published to Teamcenter by the collaborating domain.

Use Case 6:
•    ECAD or MCAD tools, in the context of a PCB Design Item, query Teamcenter to find the Eda0IDXResponse object, which was published to Teamcenter by the collaborating domain.

Use Case 7:
•    Active Workspace shows a list of ChangeItem objects for a PCB Design participating in the ECAD MCAD Collaboration.

ParameterInfo.description: This structure represents the input to the operation.

Operation.description:
ECAD MCAD Collaboration is a process of exchange of incremental design changes between ECAD and MCAD engineers, during the design process for a Printed Circuit Board (PCB).

This operation helps MCAD tool to publish PCB parent assembly BOM context for an MCAD design ItemRevision to an ECAD MCAD Collaboration. This operation also publishes an assembly file which contains the placement coordinates of the 3D models associated with the PCB parent assembly BOM context.
Operation.name: publishAssemblyContext
Operation.returnDescription:
UID and FMS Write Ticket for the Dataset containing the Assembly File.

Any failures will be returned in the ServiceData as list of partial errors:
249019: The input design is invalid.
249039: The design is not participating in any collaboration.
249044: Input bomlines cannot be empty.
Operation.useCase:
•    MCAD designer loads and visualizes the PCB parent assembly context in the tool.
•    MCAD designer can selectively disable visualization of certain parts in the BOM by unchecking the checkbox against the parts.

Operation.description:
This operation creates a new Eda0IDX business object in Teamcenter for a Design participating in an ECAD MCAD Collaboration.

ECAD MCAD Collaboration is a process of exchange of incremental design changes between ECAD and MCAD engineers, during the design process for a Printed Circuit Board (PCB). The IDX file format is a STEP (STandard for the Exchange of Product model data) based neutral format that stores electromechanical data using an XML schema.

In Teamcenter, IDX files for an ECAD MCAD Collaboration are managed as Eda0IDXBaseline, Eda0IDXIncrement and Eda0IDXResponse objects, which are types of Eda0IDX business object.

Eda0IDX represents any Teamcenter managed IDX object.

Eda0IDXBaseline represents the IDX Baseline object in ECAD MCAD Collaboration. The IDX Baseline object represents an IDX file, in which the board outline and the complete geometry information of the PCB is captured. The IDX Baseline is the agreed state of a PCB design, starting from which the ECAD and MCAD tools would make incremental changes and collaborate with each other in the PCB development process.

Eda0IDXIncrement represents an IDX (Interdomain Design Exchange) file which contains the incremental changes in a PCB layout, during the collaboration between ECAD and MCAD, as a part of PCB development process.

Eda0IDXResponse represents an IDX (Interdomain Design Exchange) file, which is created as a part of ECAD or MCAD tool reviewing and accepting or rejecting the proposed incremental changes. The IDX Response file is then imported by the tool which initiated the proposal, to bring the design in sync in both ECAD and MCAD tools.
Operation.name: publishIDX
Operation.returnDescription:
The created Eda0IDX object. Any failures will be returned with the client id mapped to error message in the ServiceData list of partial errors:
•    249005: The IDX object cannot be published. Please register publish intent before publishing.
•    249014: IDX Response cannot be published, because the IDX Increment is not downloaded for review. Please download the IDX Increment for processing.
•    249029: Input value cannot be empty. Please provide valid values for "domainIdentifier", "fileName", "fileExtension", "folderPath".
•    249030: The value provided for "idxObjectType" is invalid. The valid values are "IDXBaseline", "IDXIncrement" or "IDXResponse".
Operation.useCase:
Use Case 1:
•    ECAD or the MCAD designer while working on the PCB design, use the export IDX Baseline functionality within the respective tool, to create Eda0IDXBaseline object in Teamcenter.
•    The preceding Eda0IDXBaseline and any Eda0IDXIncrement business objects published by the calling domain, if existing, are marked as obsoleted in Teamcenter.

Use Case 2:
•    ECAD or the MCAD designer while working on the PCB design, use the export IDX Increment functionality within the respective tool, to create a Eda0IDXIncrement object in Teamcenter.

Use Case 3:
•    ECAD or the MCAD designer while working on the PCB design in their respective tool, imports an IDX Increment for reviewing the changes.
The designer reviews the changes contained within the IDX file, accepts or rejects each change and publishes an IDX Response to create an Eda0IDXResponse object in Teamcenter.

OperationTemplate.description:
This operation registers the intent of publishing an Eda0IDXBaseline or Eda0IDXIncrement object to Teamcenter by ECAD or MCAD domain, during the ECAD MCAD Collaboration process. This registration ensures that only the calling domain can publish the Eda0IDXBaseline or Eda0IDXIncrement objects to Teamcenter during the collaboration. If the intent to publish an Eda0IDXBaseline is successfully registered by one of the domains by calling this operation, the collaborating domain calling this operation would get a failure. If the intent to publish an Eda0IDXIncrement is successfully registered by one of the domains calling this operation, the collaborating domain calling this operation would get a success, as both domains can publish their respective Eda0IDXIncrement objects to Teamcenter, at the same time.

OperationTemplate.returnDescription:
ServiceData containing partial errors or warnings, if any. No errors in ServiceData indicates that the operation is successful. The list of partial errors and warnings are:
•    249019: The intent for publishing IDX Object cannot be registered because design is invalid.
•    249020: The intent for publishing IDX Object cannot be registered because intent is invalid. The valid values are ‘IDXBaseline’ or ‘IDXIncrement’.
•    249021: The intent for publishing IDX Object cannot be registered because no IDX Baseline is associated with the input design.
•    249022: The intent for publishing IDX Object cannot be registered because IDX Baseline is reserved by other domain.
•    249023: The intent for publishing IDX Object cannot be registered because the preceding IDX increment from the same domain is being processed.
•    249024: The intent for publishing IDX Object cannot be registered because the preceding IDX increment from the same domain has an IDX response to be processed. Please download the IDX Response for processing.
•    249025: The intent for publishing IDX Object cannot be registered because there is an unprocessed IDX Increment from other domain which is not processed yet. Please download the IDX Increment for processing.
•    249026: The intent for publishing IDX Object registered successfully. However, there exists an IDX Increment published by your domain, which would be marked obsolete, if you proceed to publish the IDX object. Please cancel the registered intent if this is not intended.
•    249027: The intent for publishing IDX Object is already registered successfully. Please proceed to publish the IDX object.
•    249028: The intent for publishing IDX Increment registered successfully. However, the IDX Baseline is not downloaded by the collaborating domain. If you choose to publish a new IDX Baseline instead of an IDX Increment please cancel publish intent and subsequently call this operation with the "IDXBaseline" as the publish intent value.
•    249029: Empty input value provided for designUID/domainIdentifier/publishIntent.

String representing the IDX object type to be published. Valid values are:
•    “IDXBaseline” – Get Eda0IDXBaseline objects from Teamcenter.
•    “IDXIncrement” – Get Eda0IDXIncrement objects from Teamcenter.
•    “IDXResponse” – Get Eda0IDXResponse objects from Teamcenter.
•    “ChangeItem” – Get ChangeItem objects from Teamcenter.
StructElement.name: idxObjectType

StructElement.description: Unique Identifier representing a domain, one each chosen by ECAD and MCAD. The value could be any non-empty string less than 128 characters, the only condition being that the value should be consistent across all service calls made by each domain. This is used to establish ownership of objects published by respective domains.
StructElement.name: domainIdentifier

StructElement.description:
Represents the filtering criteria, based on which the IDX objects would be returned by the operation. Valid values are:
•    Empty string – Returns the non-processed and non-obsoleted IDX objects. Applicable only when idxObjectType value is IDXBaseline or IDXIncrement.
•    includeObsolete – Returns the obsoleted IDX objects as well as the non-obsolete IDX objects. Applicable only when idxObjectType value is IDXBaseline or     IDXIncrement.
•    includePendingReview - Returns the Eda0IDXIncrement or Eda0IDXResponse, which needs to be reviewed by the caller. Applicable only when idxObjectType value is IDXIncrement or IDXResponse.
StructElement.name: filterCriteria
